# cockroach_provisioned_user (Resource)

A CockroachDB Cloud console user managed via the SCIM 2.0 user endpoints. This is the same record an identity provider creates when autoprovisioning a user on SSO login; this resource lets terraform pre-provision (or otherwise own) that record directly. Roles for the user are granted independently via the [cockroach_user_role_grant](user_role_grant) resource. Coexistence with an IdP-driven SCIM sync against the same organization is not supported — terraform is assumed to be the sole writer for any user it manages.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "cockroach_provisioned_user" "alice" {
  user_name    = "alice@example.com"
  display_name = "Alice Example"
  active       = true

  emails = [
    {
      value   = "alice@example.com"
      primary = true
      type    = "work"
    },
  ]

  name = {
    given_name  = "Alice"
    family_name = "Example"
  }
}

# Grant the new user a role. SCIM groups are not managed by this resource;
# use cockroach_user_role_grant for CockroachDB Cloud native roles.
resource "cockroach_user_role_grant" "alice_org_member" {
  user_id = cockroach_provisioned_user.alice.id
  role = {
    role_name     = "ORG_MEMBER"
    resource_type = "ORGANIZATION"
    resource_id   = ""
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `emails` (Attributes List) Email addresses for the user. At least one is required by the SCIM endpoint. Entries are stored and compared in alphabetical order by `value`, so ordering in configuration does not matter. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--emails))

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether the user is active. Setting to `false` disables sign-in without deleting the record. Defaults to `true`.
- `display_name` (String) Human-readable display name shown in the CC console.
- `external_id` (String) Identifier for the user assigned by the provisioning client (typically the IdP). Useful when bridging to an external identity store.
- `name` (Attributes) Structured name of the user. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--name))
- `user_name` (String) Login identifier for the user, typically the primary email address. The SCIM endpoint does not support renaming, so changing this forces resource replacement.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) ISO 8601 timestamp at which the user record was created on the server.
- `id` (String) Server-assigned SCIM user ID (UUID).
- `last_updated_at` (String) ISO 8601 timestamp at which the user record was last modified on the server.

<a id="nestedatt--emails"></a>
### Nested Schema for `emails`

Required:

- `value` (String) The email address.

Optional:

- `display` (String) Human-readable display value for the address.
- `primary` (Boolean) Whether this address is the user's primary email. At most one entry should set this to `true`.
- `type` (String) A category label for the address, for example `work` or `home`.


<a id="nestedatt--name"></a>
### Nested Schema for `name`

Optional:

- `family_name` (String) The user's family (last) name.
- `given_name` (String) The user's given (first) name.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# format: <resource> <SCIM user id>
terraform import cockroach_provisioned_user.alice 1f69fdd2-600a-4cfc-a9ba-16995df0d77d
```
